contentBlocks.original: (ब्रह्मवैवर्त महापुराणान्तर्गतं)
contentBlocks.transliteration: (brahmavaivarta mahāpurāṇāntargataṃ)
contentBlocks.translation: From the Brahmavaivarta Mahāpurāṇa.
contentBlocks.meanings: brahmavaivarta mahāpurāṇa-antargataṃ — contained within/belonging to the Brahmavaivarta Mahāpurāṇa.

contentBlocks.original:
भृगुरुवाच ।
ब्रह्मन्ब्रह्मविदांश्रेष्ठ ब्रह्मज्ञानविशारद ।
सर्वज्ञ सर्वजनक सर्वपूजकपूजित ॥ 60
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhṛguruvācha ।
brahmanbrahmavidāṃśrēṣṭha brahmajñānaviśārada ।
sarvajña sarvajanaka sarvapūjakapūjita ॥ 60
contentBlocks.translation: Bhṛgu said: O Brahman, O most excellent among knowers of Brahman, O expert in Brahma-knowledge, O all-knowing, O father of all, O one worshipped by all worshippers —
contentBlocks.meanings: bhṛgur uvācha — Bhṛgu said; brahman — O Brahman (addressing Brahmā); brahma-vidāṃ śrēṣṭha — O most excellent among knowers of Brahman; brahma-jñāna-viśārada — O expert/skilled in Brahma-knowledge; sarvajña — O all-knowing; sarva-janaka — O father/progenitor of all; sarva-pūjaka-pūjita — O one worshipped by all who worship.

contentBlocks.original:
सरस्वत्याश्च कवचं ब्रूहि विश्वजयं प्रभो ।
अयातयाममन्त्राणां समूहो यत्र संयुतः ॥ 61 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarasvatyāścha kavachaṃ brūhi viśvajayaṃ prabhō ।
ayātayāmamantrāṇāṃ samūhō yatra saṃyutaḥ ॥ 61 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Please tell me the kavacham of Sarasvatī that conquers the world, O Lord — the one that contains the assembly of fresh, undepleted mantras.
contentBlocks.meanings: sarasvatyāḥ cha kavachaṃ — the kavacham (armor-hymn) of Sarasvatī; brūhi — please tell; viśva-jayaṃ — that conquers the world; prabhō — O Lord; ayāta-yāma-mantrāṇāṃ — of mantras that are ayāta-yāma (fresh, not stale from previous use, full of undiminished power); samūhaḥ — assembly/collection; yatra saṃyutaḥ — where combined.

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मोवाच ।
शृणु वत्स प्रवक्ष्यामि कवचं सर्वकामदम् ।
श्रुतिसारं श्रुतिसुखं श्रुत्युक्तं श्रुतिपूजितम् ॥ 62 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmōvācha ।
śṛṇu vatsa pravakṣyāmi kavachaṃ sarvakāmadam ।
śrutisāraṃ śrutisukhaṃ śrutyuktaṃ śrutipūjitam ॥ 62 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Brahmā said: Listen, O dear son, I will tell you the kavacham that grants all desires — the essence of scripture, pleasing to scripture, declared by scripture, honored by scripture.
contentBlocks.meanings: brahmā uvācha — Brahmā said; śṛṇu vatsa — listen, O dear son; pravakṣyāmi — I will declare/tell; kavachaṃ — the protective armor-hymn; sarva-kāma-dam — granting all desires; śruti-sāraṃ — essence of the Vedas; śruti-sukhaṃ — pleasing to the Vedas; śruty-uktaṃ — declared by the Vedas; śruti-pūjitam — honored by the Vedas.

contentBlocks.original:
उक्तं कृष्णेन गोलोके मह्यं वृन्दावने वने ।
रासेश्वरेण विभुना रासे वै रासमण्डले ॥ 63 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
uktaṃ kṛṣṇēna gōlōkē mahyaṃ vṛndāvanē vanē ।
rāsēśvarēṇa vibhunā rāsē vai rāsamaṇḍalē ॥ 63 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Declared by Kṛṣṇa in Goloka, to me in Vṛndāvana forest — by the Lord of the Rāsa dance, the all-pervading one, during the Rāsa circle.
contentBlocks.meanings: uktaṃ kṛṣṇēna — declared by Kṛṣṇa; gōlōkē — in Goloka (Kṛṣṇa's eternal abode); mahyaṃ — to me (Brahmā); vṛndāvanē vanē — in Vṛndāvana forest; rāsēśvarēṇa — by the Lord of the Rāsa; vibhunā — the all-pervading one; rāsē vai rāsa-maṇḍalē — in the Rāsa [dance], in the Rāsa circle.

contentBlocks.original:
अतीव गोपनीयञ्च कल्पवृक्षसमं परम् ।
अश्रुताद्भुतमन्त्राणां समूहैश्च समन्वितम् ॥ 64 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atīva gōpanīyañcha kalpavṛkṣasamaṃ param ।
aśrutādbhutamantrāṇāṃ samūhaiścha samanvitam ॥ 64 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Most secret, equal to the wish-granting tree, supremely excellent — endowed with clusters of unheard and wondrous mantras.
contentBlocks.meanings: atīva gōpanīyaṃ — most secret; kalpa-vṛkṣa-samaṃ — equal to the wish-granting tree (kalpavṛkṣa); paraṃ — supreme; aśruta-adbhuta-mantrāṇāṃ — of mantras that are unheard/unprecedented and wondrous; samūhaiḥ cha samanvitam — endowed with/accompanied by groups.

contentBlocks.original:
यद्धृत्वा पठनाद्ब्रह्मन्बुद्धिमांश्च बृहस्पतिः ।
यद्धृत्वा भगवाञ्छुक्रः सर्वदैत्येषु पूजितः ॥ 65 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yaddhṛtvā paṭhanādbrahmanbuddhimāṃścha bṛhaspatiḥ ।
yaddhṛtvā bhagavāñChukraḥ sarvadaityēṣu pūjitaḥ ॥ 65 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: By wearing and reciting which, O Brahmin, Bṛhaspati became supremely intelligent; by wearing which, Bhagavān Śukra became worshipped among all the demons.
contentBlocks.meanings: yad-dhṛtvā — by wearing which; paṭhanāt — by reciting; brahman — O Brahmin; buddhi-māṃ — supremely intelligent; bṛhaspatiḥ — Bṛhaspati (guru of the gods); yad-dhṛtvā — by wearing which; bhagavān śukraḥ — Bhagavān Śukra (guru of the demons); sarva-daityēṣu pūjitaḥ — worshipped among all the demons.

contentBlocks.original:
पठनाद्धारणाद्वाग्मी कवीन्द्रो वाल्मिकी मुनिः ।
स्वायम्भुवो मनुश्चैव यद्धृत्वा सर्वपूजिताः ॥ 66 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
paṭhanāddhāraṇādvāgmī kavīndrō vālmikī muniḥ ।
svāyambhuvō manuśchaiva yaddhṛtvā sarvapūjitāḥ ॥ 66 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: By reciting and wearing which, Vālmīki the sage became eloquent and chief of poets; by wearing which, Svāyambhuva Manu and others became universally worshipped.
contentBlocks.meanings: paṭhanāt dhāraṇāt — by reciting and wearing/retaining; vāgmī — eloquent/skilled in speech; kavīndraḥ — chief of poets; vālmīkī muniḥ — the sage Vālmīki; svāyambhuvō manuḥ cha eva — Svāyambhuva Manu as well; yad-dhṛtvā — by wearing which; sarva-pūjitāḥ — universally worshipped.

contentBlocks.original:
कणादो गौतमः कण्वः पाणिनिः शाकटायनः ।
ग्रन्थं चकार यद्धृत्वा दक्षः कात्यायनः स्वयम् ॥ 67 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaṇādō gautamaḥ kaṇvaḥ pāṇiniḥ śākaṭāyanaḥ ।
granthaṃ chakāra yaddhṛtvā dakṣaḥ kātyāyanaḥ svayam ॥ 67 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Kaṇāda, Gautama, Kaṇva, Pāṇini, Śākaṭāyana — by wearing which, Dakṣa and Kātyāyana himself also composed their texts.
contentBlocks.meanings: kaṇādaḥ — Kaṇāda (founder of Vaiśeṣika philosophy); gautamaḥ — Gautama (founder of Nyāya logic); kaṇvaḥ — Kaṇva (Vedic sage and grammarian); pāṇiniḥ — Pāṇini (great Sanskrit grammarian, author of Aṣṭādhyāyī); śākaṭāyanaḥ — Śākaṭāyana (early grammarian); granthaṃ chakāra — composed their text; yad-dhṛtvā — by wearing which; dakṣaḥ kātyāyanaḥ svayam — Dakṣa and Kātyāyana himself.

contentBlocks.original:
धृत्वा वेदविभागञ्च पुराणान्यखिलानि च ।
चकार लीलामात्रेण कृष्णद्वैपायनः स्वयम् ॥ 68 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhṛtvā vēdavibhāgañcha purāṇānyakhilāni cha ।
chakāra līlāmātrēṇa kṛṣṇadvaipāyanaḥ svayam ॥ 68 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Having worn which, having divided the Vedas and composed all the Purāṇas — Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa) accomplished all this as mere play.
contentBlocks.meanings: dhṛtvā — having worn; vēda-vibhāgaṃ cha — the division of the Vedas; purāṇāni akhilāni cha — and all the Purāṇas; chakāra līlā-mātrēṇa — accomplished as mere play/sport; kṛṣṇa-dvaipāyanaḥ svayam — Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana (Vyāsa) himself.

contentBlocks.original:
शातातपश्च संवर्तो वसिष्ठश्च पराशरः ।
यद्धृत्वा पठनाद्ग्रन्थं याज्ञवल्क्यश्चकार सः ॥ 69 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śātātapaścha saṃvartō vasiṣṭhaścha parāśaraḥ ।
yaddhṛtvā paṭhanādgranthaṃ yājñavalkyaśchakāra saḥ ॥ 69 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Śātātapa, Saṃvarta, Vasiṣṭha, and Parāśara — by wearing and reciting which, Yājñavalkya too composed his texts.
contentBlocks.meanings: śātātapaḥ — Śātātapa (Smṛti author); saṃvartaḥ — Saṃvarta (Dharmaśāstra sage); vasiṣṭhaḥ — Vasiṣṭha (one of the seven great sages); parāśaraḥ — Parāśara (father of Vyāsa, author of Parāśara Smṛti and Viṣṇu Purāṇa); yad-dhṛtvā paṭhanāt — by wearing and reciting which; granthaṃ chakāra saḥ — he composed his texts; yājñavalkyaḥ — Yājñavalkya (the great sage of Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad and Yoga-yājñavalkya).

contentBlocks.original:
ऋष्यशृङ्गो भरद्वाजश्चास्तीको देवलस्तथा ।
जैगीषव्योऽथ जाबालिर्यद्धृत्वा सर्वपूजितः ॥ 70 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ṛṣyaśṛṅgō bharadvājaśchāstīkō dēvalastathā ।
jaigīṣavyō'tha jābāliryaddhṛtvā sarvapūjitaḥ ॥ 70 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Ṛṣyaśṛṅga, Bharadvāja, Āstīka, Devala, Jaigīṣavya, and Jābāli — by wearing which, they became universally worshipped.
contentBlocks.meanings: ṛṣyaśṛṅgaḥ — Ṛṣyaśṛṅga (sage famous in Rāmāyaṇa); bharadvājaḥ cha — and Bharadvāja (author of many Vedic hymns and Āyurveda texts); āstīkaḥ — Āstīka (sage who stopped the serpent sacrifice); dēvalaḥ — Devala (Dharmaśāstra sage); jaigīṣavyaḥ — Jaigīṣavya (Sāṃkhya sage); jābāliḥ — Jābāli (Vedic sage); yad-dhṛtvā — by wearing which; sarva-pūjitaḥ — became universally revered.

contentBlocks.original:
कवचस्यास्य विप्रेन्द्र ऋषिरेष प्रजापतिः ।
स्वयं बृहस्पतिश्छन्दो देवो रासेश्वरः प्रभुः ॥ 71 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kavachasyāsya viprēndra ṛṣirēṣa prajāpatiḥ ।
svayaṃ bṛhaspatiśChandō dēvō rāsēśvaraḥ prabhuḥ ॥ 71 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Of this kavacham, O best of Brahmins, the seer is Prajāpati (Brahmā himself); the meter is Bṛhaspati himself; and the presiding deity is the Lord of the Rāsa (Kṛṣṇa).
contentBlocks.meanings: kavachasya asya — of this kavacham; viprēndra — O best of Brahmins; ṛṣiḥ ēṣaḥ prajāpatiḥ — the seer is Prajāpati (Brahmā); svayaṃ bṛhaspatiḥ chandaḥ — the meter is Bṛhaspati himself; dēvaḥ rāsēśvaraḥ prabhuḥ — the deity is the Lord of the Rāsa (Kṛṣṇa).

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वतत्त्वपरिज्ञाने सर्वार्थेऽपि च साधने ।
कवितासु च सर्वासु विनियोगः प्रकीर्तितः ॥ 72 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvatattvaparijñānē sarvārthē'pi cha sādhanē ।
kavitāsu cha sarvāsu viniyōgaḥ prakīrtitaḥ ॥ 72 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The application [of this kavacham] is declared to be: for knowing all tattvas, for accomplishing all aims, and for all forms of poetry.
contentBlocks.meanings: sarva-tattva-parijñānē — for complete knowledge of all tattvas (cosmic principles); sarva-arthē api cha sādhanē — and also for accomplishing all purposes/aims; kavitāsu cha sarvāsu — and for all forms of poetry; viniyōgaḥ prakīrtitaḥ — the application is declared/renowned.

contentBlocks.original:
( कवचं )
ॐ ह्रीं सरस्वत्यै स्वाहा शिरो मे पातु सर्वतः ।
श्रीं वाग्देवतायै स्वाहा भालं मे सर्वदाऽवतु ॥ 73 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
( kavachaṃ )
ōṃ hrīṃ sarasvatyai svāhā śirō mē pātu sarvataḥ ।
śrīṃ vāgdēvatāyai svāhā bhālaṃ mē sarvadā'vatu ॥ 73 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃ Hrīṃ Sarasvatyai Svāhā — may [she] protect my head in all directions. Śrīṃ Vāgdēvatāyai Svāhā — may [she] ever protect my forehead.
contentBlocks.meanings: kavachaṃ — the armor-hymn; ōṃ hrīṃ sarasvatyai svāhā — the primary mantra of Sarasvatī with Hrīṃ (māyā-bīja) and Svāhā; śiraḥ mē pātu sarvataḥ — may [she] protect my head from all directions; śrīṃ vāg-dēvatāyai svāhā — Śrīṃ [bīja], O Goddess of Speech, Svāhā; bhālaṃ mē sarvadā avatu — may [she] ever protect my forehead.

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ ह्रीं सरस्वत्यै स्वाहेति श्रोत्रे पातु निरन्तरम् ।
ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं भगवत्यै सरस्वत्यै स्वाहा नेत्रयुग्मं सदाऽवतु ॥ 74 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṃ hrīṃ sarasvatyai svāhēti śrōtrē pātu nirantaram ।
ōṃ śrīṃ hrīṃ bhagavatyai sarasvatyai svāhā nētrayugmaṃ sadā'vatu ॥ 74 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃ Hrīṃ Sarasvatyai Svāhā — may [she] ever protect my ears. Oṃ Śrīṃ Hrīṃ Bhagavatyai Sarasvatyai Svāhā — may [she] ever protect my two eyes.
contentBlocks.meanings: śrōtrē pātu nirantaram — protect the ears continuously; ōṃ śrīṃ hrīṃ bhagavatyai sarasvatyai svāhā — combined three-bīja mantra with Bhagavatī Sarasvatī; nētra-yugmaṃ sadā avatu — may [she] ever protect both eyes.

contentBlocks.original:
ऐं ह्रीं वाग्वादिन्यै स्वाहा नासां मे सर्वदाऽवतु ।
ह्रीं विद्याधिष्ठातृदेव्यै स्वाहा चोंष्ठ सदाऽवतु ॥ 75 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aiṃ hrīṃ vāgvādinyai svāhā nāsāṃ mē sarvadā'vatu ।
hrīṃ vidyādhiṣṭhātṛdēvyai svāhā chōṃṣṭha sadā'vatu ॥ 75 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Aiṃ Hrīṃ Vāgvādinyai Svāhā — may [she] ever protect my nose. Hrīṃ Vidyādhiṣṭhātṛdēvyai Svāhā — may [she] ever protect my lips.
contentBlocks.meanings: aiṃ hrīṃ vāg-vādinyai svāhā — Aiṃ (the Sarasvatī/vāk bīja), Hrīṃ, to Vāgvādinī (she who speaks eloquently), Svāhā; nāsāṃ mē sarvadā avatu — ever protect my nose; hrīṃ vidyā-adhiṣṭhātṛ-dēvyai svāhā — Hrīṃ, to the Goddess who presides over knowledge/learning; ōṃṣṭha sadā avatu — ever protect the lips.

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं ब्राह्म्यै स्वाहेति दन्तपङ्क्तिं सदाऽवतु ।
ऐमित्येकाक्षरो मन्त्रो मम कण्ठं सदाऽवतु ॥ 76 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṃ śrīṃ hrīṃ brāhmyai svāhēti dantapaṅktiṃ sadā'vatu ।
aimityēkākṣarō mantrō mama kaṇṭhaṃ sadā'vatu ॥ 76 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃ Śrīṃ Hrīṃ Brāhmyai Svāhā — may [she] ever protect my row of teeth. Aiṃ — this single-syllable mantra — may [she] ever protect my throat.
contentBlocks.meanings: ōṃ śrīṃ hrīṃ brāhmyai svāhā — triple-bīja mantra with Brāhmī (the Śakti of Brahmā); danta-paṅktiṃ sadā avatu — ever protect the row of teeth; aim iti ēkākṣaraḥ mantraḥ — Aiṃ, this one-syllable mantra; mama kaṇṭhaṃ sadā avatu — ever protect my throat.

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं पातु मे ग्रीवां स्कन्धं मे श्रीं सदाऽवतु ।
ॐ ह्रीं विद्याधिष्ठातृदेव्यै स्वाहा वक्षः सदाऽवतु ॥ 77 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṃ śrīṃ hrīṃ pātu mē grīvāṃ skandhaṃ mē śrīṃ sadā'vatu ।
ōṃ hrīṃ vidyādhiṣṭhātṛdēvyai svāhā vakṣaḥ sadā'vatu ॥ 77 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃ Śrīṃ Hrīṃ — may [she] protect my neck; Śrīṃ — may [she] ever protect my shoulders. Oṃ Hrīṃ Vidyādhiṣṭhātṛdēvyai Svāhā — may [she] ever protect my chest.
contentBlocks.meanings: ōṃ śrīṃ hrīṃ pātu mē grīvāṃ — Oṃ Śrīṃ Hrīṃ, protect my neck; skandhaṃ mē śrīṃ sadā avatu — Śrīṃ, ever protect my shoulders; ōṃ hrīṃ vidyā-adhiṣṭhātṛ-dēvyai svāhā — the mantra of the Goddess of learning; vakṣaḥ sadā avatu — ever protect the chest.

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ ह्रीं विद्यास्वरूपायै स्वाहा मे पातु नाभिकाम् ।
ॐ ह्रीं क्लीं वाण्यै स्वाहेति मम पृष्ठं सदाऽवतु ॥ 78 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṃ hrīṃ vidyāsvarūpāyai svāhā mē pātu nābhikām ।
ōṃ hrīṃ klīṃ vāṇyai svāhēti mama pṛṣṭhaṃ sadā'vatu ॥ 78 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃ Hrīṃ Vidyāsvarūpāyai Svāhā — may [she] protect my navel. Oṃ Hrīṃ Klīṃ Vāṇyai Svāhā — may [she] ever protect my back.
contentBlocks.meanings: ōṃ hrīṃ vidyā-svarūpāyai svāhā — to the one whose very form is knowledge; nābhikāṃ mē pātu — protect my navel; ōṃ hrīṃ klīṃ vāṇyai svāhā — three bījas with Vāṇī (Sarasvatī as speech); mama pṛṣṭhaṃ sadā avatu — ever protect my back.

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ सर्ववर्णात्मिकायै पादयुग्मं सदाऽवतु ।
ॐ रागाधिष्ठातृदेव्यै सर्वाङ्गं मे सदाऽवतु ॥ 79 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṃ sarvavarṇātmikāyai pādayugmaṃ sadā'vatu ।
ōṃ rāgādhiṣṭhātṛdēvyai sarvāṅgaṃ mē sadā'vatu ॥ 79 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃ Sarvavarṇātmikāyai — may [she] ever protect my two feet. Oṃ Rāgādhiṣṭhātṛdēvyai — may [she] ever protect all my limbs.
contentBlocks.meanings: ōṃ sarva-varṇa-ātmikāyai — to the one who is the soul/essence of all letters/sounds; pāda-yugmaṃ sadā avatu — ever protect both feet; ōṃ rāga-adhiṣṭhātṛ-dēvyai — to the Goddess who presides over rāga (musical mode/love); sarva-aṅgaṃ mē sadā avatu — ever protect all my limbs.

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ सर्वकण्ठवासिन्यै स्वाहा प्रच्यां सदाऽवतु ।
ॐ ह्रीं जिह्वाग्रवासिन्यै स्वाहाऽग्निदिशि रक्षतु ॥ 80 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṃ sarvakaṇṭhavāsinyai svāhā prachyāṃ sadā'vatu ।
ōṃ hrīṃ jihvāgravāsinyai svāhā'gnidiśi rakṣatu ॥ 80 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃ Sarvakaṇṭhavāsinyai Svāhā — may [she] ever protect the East. Oṃ Hrīṃ Jihvāgravāsinyai Svāhā — may [she] protect the south-east (fire-direction).
contentBlocks.meanings: ōṃ sarva-kaṇṭha-vāsinyai svāhā — to the one who dwells in all throats; prachyāṃ sadā avatu — may [she] ever protect the East; ōṃ hrīṃ jihvā-agra-vāsinyai svāhā — to the one who dwells at the tip of the tongue; agni-diśi rakṣatu — may [she] protect in the fire-direction (south-east).

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ ऐं ह्रीं श्रीं सरस्वत्यै बुधजनन्यै स्वाहा ।
सततं मन्त्रराजोऽयं दक्षिणे मां सदाऽवतु ॥ 81 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṃ aiṃ hrīṃ śrīṃ sarasvatyai budhajananyai svāhā ।
satataṃ mantrarājō'yaṃ dakṣiṇē māṃ sadā'vatu ॥ 81 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃ Aiṃ Hrīṃ Śrīṃ Sarasvatyai Budhajananyai Svāhā — this king of mantras, may [she] ever protect me in the South.
contentBlocks.meanings: ōṃ aiṃ hrīṃ śrīṃ sarasvatyai — the triple-bīja Sarasvatī mantra (Aiṃ Hrīṃ Śrīṃ); budha-jananyai — to the mother of the wise/learned (Budha = Mercury, also 'learned person'); svāhā — offering; satataṃ mantra-rājaḥ ayaṃ — this is the king of mantras; dakṣiṇē māṃ sadā avatu — may [it/she] ever protect me in the South.

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ ह्रीं श्रीं त्र्यक्षरो मन्त्रो नैरृत्यां मे सदाऽवतु ।
कविजिह्वाग्रवासिन्यै स्वाहा मां वारुणेऽवतु ॥ 82 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṃ hrīṃ śrīṃ tryakṣarō mantrō nairṛtyāṃ mē sadā'vatu ।
kavijihvāgravāsinyai svāhā māṃ vāruṇē'vatu ॥ 82 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃ Hrīṃ Śrīṃ — the three-syllable mantra — may [she] ever protect me in the south-west. Kavijihvāgravāsinyai Svāhā — may [she] protect me in the West.
contentBlocks.meanings: ōṃ hrīṃ śrīṃ — the three-syllable mantra; tryakṣaraḥ mantraḥ — the three-syllable/letter mantra; nairṛtyāṃ mē sadā avatu — ever protect me in the south-west (Nirṛti direction); kavi-jihvā-agra-vāsinyai svāhā — to the one who dwells at the tip of poets' tongues; māṃ vāruṇē avatu — protect me in the West (Varuṇa's direction).

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ सदम्बिकायै स्वाहा वायव्ये मां सदाऽवतु ।
ॐ गद्यपद्यवासिन्यै स्वाहा मामुत्तरेऽवतु ॥ 83 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṃ sadambikāyai svāhā vāyavyē māṃ sadā'vatu ।
ōṃ gadyapadyavāsinyai svāhā māmuttarē'vatu ॥ 83 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃ Sadambikāyai Svāhā — may [she] ever protect me in the north-west. Oṃ Gadyapadyavāsinyai Svāhā — may [she] protect me in the North.
contentBlocks.meanings: ōṃ sad-ambikāyai svāhā — to the always-good/eternal Mother; vāyavyē māṃ sadā avatu — ever protect me in the north-west (Vāyu's direction); ōṃ gadya-padya-vāsinyai svāhā — to the one who dwells in prose and verse; māṃ uttarē avatu — protect me in the North.

contentBlocks.original:
ॐ सर्वशास्त्रवासिन्यै स्वाहैशान्यां सदाऽवतु ।
ॐ ह्रीं सर्वपूजितायै स्वाहा चोर्ध्वं सदाऽवतु ॥ 84 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ōṃ sarvaśāstravāsinyai svāhaiśānyāṃ sadā'vatu ।
ōṃ hrīṃ sarvapūjitāyai svāhā chōrdhvaṃ sadā'vatu ॥ 84 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Oṃ Sarvaśāstravāsinyai Svāhā — may [she] ever protect in the north-east. Oṃ Hrīṃ Sarvapūjitāyai Svāhā — may [she] ever protect from above.
contentBlocks.meanings: ōṃ sarva-śāstra-vāsinyai svāhā — to the one who dwells in all śāstras (scriptures); aiśānyāṃ sadā avatu — ever protect in the north-east (Īśāna/Śiva's direction); ōṃ hrīṃ sarva-pūjitāyai svāhā — to the one worshipped by all; cha ūrdhvaṃ sadā avatu — may [she] ever protect from above.

contentBlocks.original:
ऐं ह्रीं पुस्तकवासिन्यै स्वाहाऽधो मां सदावतु ।
ॐ ग्रन्थबीजरूपायै स्वाहा मां सर्वतोऽवतु ॥ 85 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aiṃ hrīṃ pustakavāsinyai svāhā'dhō māṃ sadāvatu ।
ōṃ granthabījarūpāyai svāhā māṃ sarvatō'vatu ॥ 85 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Aiṃ Hrīṃ Pustakavāsinyai Svāhā — may [she] ever protect me below. Oṃ Granthābījarūpāyai Svāhā — may [she] protect me in all directions.
contentBlocks.meanings: aiṃ hrīṃ pustaka-vāsinyai svāhā — to the one who dwells in books/sacred texts; adhaḥ māṃ sadā avatu — ever protect me below; ōṃ grantha-bīja-rūpāyai svāhā — to the one whose form is the seed/origin of all texts; māṃ sarvataḥ avatu — may [she] protect me in all directions.

contentBlocks.original:
इति ते कथितं विप्र सर्वमन्त्रौघविग्रहम् ।
इदं विश्वजयं नाम कवचं ब्रह्मारूपकम् ॥ 86 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti tē kathitaṃ vipra sarvamantraughavigraham ।
idaṃ viśvajayaṃ nāma kavachaṃ brahmārūpakam ॥ 86 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus, O Brahmin, have I told you the embodiment of all mantra-hosts — this world-conquering kavacham which is in the form of Brahmā himself.
contentBlocks.meanings: iti — thus; tē kathitaṃ — have I told you; vipra — O Brahmin; sarva-mantra-ōgha-vigraham — the embodiment/form of the flood/mass of all mantras; idaṃ viśva-jayaṃ nāma — this, named 'world-conquering'; kavachaṃ brahma-rūpakam — the kavacham that is the form of Brahmā.

contentBlocks.original:
पुरा श्रुतं धर्मवक्त्रात्पर्वते गन्धमादने ।
तव स्नेहान्मयाऽऽख्यातं प्रवक्तव्यं न कस्यचित् ॥ 87 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
purā śrutaṃ dharmavaktrātparvatē gandhamādanē ।
tava snēhānmayā''khyātaṃ pravaktavyaṃ na kasyachit ॥ 87 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: In former times, I heard this from Dharma-lord on the Gandhamādana mountain. Out of love for you I have disclosed it — it must not be told to just anyone.
contentBlocks.meanings: purā — in former times; śrutaṃ — heard; dharma-vaktrāt — from the mouth of Dharma-lord; parvatē gandhamādanē — on Gandhamādana mountain; tava snēhāt — out of love/affection for you; mayā ākhyātaṃ — I have disclosed/told; pravaktavyaṃ na kasyachit — must not be told to just anyone.

contentBlocks.original:
गुरुमभ्यर्च्य विधिवद्वस्त्रालङ्कारचन्दनैः ।
प्रणम्य दण्डवद्भूमौ कवचं धारयेत्सुधीः ॥ 88 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
gurumabhyarchya vidhivadvastrālaṅkārachandanaiḥ ।
praṇamya daṇḍavadbhūmau kavachaṃ dhārayētsudhīḥ ॥ 88 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: The wise one should duly worship the guru with garments, ornaments, and sandalwood; prostrate flat on the ground like a staff; then wear the kavacham.
contentBlocks.meanings: gurum abhyarchya — having duly worshipped the guru; vidhivat — in the proper manner; vastra-alaṅkāra-chandanaiḥ — with garments, ornaments, and sandalwood; praṇamya daṇḍavat bhūmau — having prostrated like a staff (daṇḍa-praṇāma) on the ground; kavachaṃ dhārayēt sudhīḥ — the wise/intelligent one should wear the kavacham.

contentBlocks.original:
पञ्चलक्षजपेनैव सिद्धं तु कवचं भवेत् ।
यदि स्यात्सिद्धकवचो बृहस्पति समो भवेत् ॥ 89 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pañchalakṣajapēnaiva siddhaṃ tu kavachaṃ bhavēt ।
yadi syātsiddhakavachō bṛhaspati samō bhavēt ॥ 89 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: By five lakh recitations, the kavacham becomes accomplished (siddha). If one has a siddha-kavacham, one becomes equal to Bṛhaspati.
contentBlocks.meanings: pañcha-lakṣa-japēna eva — by exactly five lakh (500,000) recitations; siddhaṃ tu kavachaṃ bhavēt — the kavacham truly becomes siddha (accomplished); yadi syāt siddha-kavachaḥ — if one has a siddha-kavacham; bṛhaspati samaḥ bhavēt — one becomes equal to Bṛhaspati.

contentBlocks.original:
महावाग्मी कवीन्द्रश्च त्रैलोक्यविजयी भवेत् ।
शक्नोति सर्वं जेतुं स कवचस्य प्रभावतः ॥ 90 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahāvāgmī kavīndraścha trailōkyavijayī bhavēt ।
śaknōti sarvaṃ jētuṃ sa kavachasya prabhāvataḥ ॥ 90 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: One becomes a great orator and chief among poets, a conqueror of all three worlds. By the power of this kavacham, such a one can conquer everything.
contentBlocks.meanings: mahā-vāgmī — great orator/supremely eloquent; kavi-indraḥ cha — and chief/lord of poets; trailōkya-vijayī — conqueror of the three worlds; bhavēt — becomes; śaknōti sarvaṃ jētuṃ saḥ — such a one is able to conquer/overcome everything; kavachasya prabhāvataḥ — by the power/glory of this kavacham.

contentBlocks.original:
इदं ते काण्वशाखोक्तं कथितं कवचं मुने ।
स्तोत्रं पूजाविधानं च ध्यानं वै वन्दनं तथा ॥ 91 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
idaṃ tē kāṇvaśākhōktaṃ kathitaṃ kavachaṃ munē ।
stōtraṃ pūjāvidhānaṃ cha dhyānaṃ vai vandanaṃ tathā ॥ 91 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O sage, this kavacham of the Kāṇva-śākhā tradition has been told to you, along with the stotram, the procedure for worship, dhyāna, and the hymn of praise.
contentBlocks.meanings: idaṃ tē kāṇva-śākhā-uktaṃ — this, of the Kāṇva-śākhā (branch of Śukla Yajurveda), told to you; kathitaṃ kavachaṃ munē — the kavacham has been told, O sage; stōtraṃ — stotram; pūjā-vidhānaṃ cha — and the procedure for worship; dhyānaṃ vai — dhyāna indeed; vandanaṃ tathā — and the hymn of praise/salutation.

contentBlocks.original: इति श्री ब्रह्मवैवर्ते महापुराणे प्रकृतिखण्डे नारदनारायणसंवादे सरस्वतीकवचं नाम चतुर्थोऽध्यायः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrī brahmavaivartē mahāpurāṇē prakṛtikhaṇḍē nāradanārāyaṇasaṃvādē sarasvatīkavachaṃ nāma chaturthō'dhyāyaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Thus [ends] the Sarasvatī Kavacham, named the Fourth Chapter, in the Nārada-Nārāyaṇa dialogue, in the Prakṛti-Khaṇḍa of the Śrī Brahmavaivarta Mahāpurāṇa.
contentBlocks.meanings: iti — thus; śrī brahma-vaivartē mahā-purāṇē — in the Śrī Brahmavaivarta Mahāpurāṇa; prakṛti-khaṇḍē — in the Prakṛti-Khaṇḍa (section on Primal Nature); nārada-nārāyaṇa-saṃvādē — in the dialogue of Nārada and Nārāyaṇa; sarasvatī-kavachaṃ nāma — named the Sarasvatī Kavacham; chaturthaḥ adhyāyaḥ — the Fourth Chapter.
